document.name: AKTI ERP Phase 3 Ticket Pack v1
document.authority: implementation contract for Phase 3 tickets; lower authority than Prisma, contracts, manifests, generated registry, ADRs, and AGENTS.md
document.audit_report_rule: Phase 3 audit report is a closure artifact only and must say: To be completed at Phase 3 closure.

p3_014_inclusion_decision.reason: Repo inspection confirms CI naming/security-gate alignment is needed and safely scoped: .github/workflows/phase1-validation.yml is Phase 1-named while the workflow name and job are Phase 2-oriented.
allowed_scope: preserving the existing Phase 1/2 validation ladder

maximum_concrete_capability.statement: Phase 3 must implement the maximum concrete security/auth/tenant capability that is justified by current repo authority and current Phase 3 scope.
means: do not choose the shallow/document-only path when runtime implementation is safely possible
do not defer work merely because it is harder
do not leave decorative contracts, placeholder policies, or untested claims
convert accepted decisions into runtime behavior, tests, and validation evidence wherever current scope permits
prefer stronger implementation over temporary documentation when the repo already has enough authority to implement safely
does_not_permit: bypassing ADRs, contracts, Prisma, manifests, or AGENTS.md

global_rules: exact-file planning is required before edits
broad globs are inspection hints only
control docs are stable contract, not runtime ledger
runtime progress comes from git commits, journal, artifacts, optional run-state, and queue order
bounded repair is allowed only inside active ticket scope
validation/test-runner wiring is allowed only when required by active-ticket tests
full-access/elevated execution exception remains bounded by active control docs and stop conditions
do not retire full-access/elevated execution exception

do not weaken Phase 1/2 protections: Access Core actor/capability boundaries, Gatekeeper fail-closed behavior, Lead Desk scope enforcement, Engagement Gateway mediated WhatsApp boundary, audit/outbox evidence, cross-org denial behavior
tests using x-actor-user-id may be updated only if replaced by equivalent or stronger trusted-context coverage
removing old header-based tests without equivalent coverage is forbidden
P3-001 through P3-006 are decision/control tickets only
P3-001 through P3-006 must not modify runtime source, Prisma schema, contracts, generated registry, dependencies, or workflow files unless reclassified and explicitly approved
Any ticket whose scope depends on an earlier decision must include conditional_replan_required: true
Any ticket with broad implementation risk must be split before execution, not repaired through broadening during execution

stale-ticket risk prevention is mandatory: tickets must encode dependency gates, runtime consistency chains, validation gates, re-planning triggers, explicit non-scope boundaries, and stop conditions before implementation starts

after_decision_or_documentation_ticket: if JSON files changed, parse them with Node before commit
after_implementation_ticket: run ticket-specific validation plus full applicable validation ladder before starting the next implementation ticket
after_schema_registry_auth_tenant_access: run full validation and drift checks
verify no previous Phase 1/2 tests or validation wiring were removed or weakened
validation_gate_policy.failure_policy: Any validation failure outside active ticket scope is a stop unless bounded repair is explicitly allowed by the ticket.

runtime_consistency_chains: auth/session decision -> request context -> controller/service migration -> tests
tenant isolation decision -> enforcement strategy -> cross-tenant negative tests
Access Core/Gatekeeper integration -> fail-closed behavior -> no-write-on-failure tests
frontend auth decision -> operator context replacement -> frontend tests
route limiting decision -> runtime or bounded deferral -> manifest/runtime alignment
fresh DB/bootstrap decision -> Phase 3 action or Phase 4 handoff
hard_boundaries: no new dependencies unless the ticket stops for explicit approval

tickets.title: Track Phase 3 controls and baseline
tickets.objective: Create the Phase 3 control baseline and confirm repo state before implementation tickets begin.
scope: confirm main branch and current baseline
confirm Phase 3 control documents exist and are tracked
confirm ticket pack queue and audit stub rules
record that Phase 3 verdict is HYBRID leaning ADDING missing security/tenant architecture

docs/process/AKTI_ERP_Phase_3_Ticket_Pack_v1.json
docs/process/AKTI_ERP_Phase_3_Audit_Report_v1.md
tests_required: search confirmations for required Phase 3 control language
stop_conditions: unexpected uncommitted files exist before starting
control docs require runtime code changes
JSON parse fails
audit report contains planning-time findings
acceptance_criteria: Phase 3 plan, ticket pack, and audit stub are tracked

audit report remains a closure-only stub

tickets.rollback_notes: Revert only the Phase 3 control-doc commit if this ticket needs rollback.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: This baseline ticket prevents stale ticket assumptions by making the queue, gates, and validation policy explicit before implementation begins.
tickets.runtime_consistency_chain: control docs -> ordered ticket queue -> validation gates -> Phase 3 implementation

tickets.title: Security Architecture ADR
tickets.objective: Create the Phase 3 security architecture decision that anchors auth, tenant isolation, Access Core, Gatekeeper, route limiting, secrets/env, and frontend operator context work.
scope: document security architecture boundaries for Phase 3
confirm which foundations are hardening versus adding
define how ADRs, Prisma, contracts, manifests, and AGENTS.md constrain implementation
confirm Phase 4 remains blocked until Phase 3 decisions close

stop_conditions: decision requires implementation detail not authorized by existing source of truth
ADR attempts to bypass Prisma, contracts, manifests, generated registry, or AGENTS.md
ticket scope expands into Phase 4 deployment or staging
acceptance_criteria: ADR exists and is decision-only
ADR states Phase 3 is HYBRID leaning ADDING missing security/tenant architecture
ADR preserves Phase 1/2 protections
ADR gates implementation tickets behind required decisions
tickets.rollback_notes: Revert the ADR commit only; no runtime artifacts should exist.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: Decision must name downstream gates so implementation tickets do not rediscover basic security boundaries mid-run.
tickets.runtime_consistency_chain: Security Architecture ADR -> Auth/Tenant decisions -> implementation ticket gates
